commit.message:
Create complete React frontend for anonymous Q&A platform

- Add package.json, .env.example, public/index.html
- Add src/index.js entry point with Redux Provider + BrowserRouter
- Add src/App.js with all nested routes (public + PrivateRoute-guarded)
- Add src/api/index.js: Axios instance with Bearer token interceptor,
  401 auto-logout, and all API functions (auth/questions/answers/users/admin)
- Add src/store/index.js and slices (authSlice, questionsSlice)
  with async thunks for login, register, fetchQuestions, fetchQuestion
- Add src/components/AppLayout.js: sticky header, collapsible sider,
  notification bell badge, user avatar dropdown, responsive mobile support
- Add src/components/PrivateRoute.js: token + role-based guard
- Add src/pages/Register.js: form with username/email/password/confirm validation
- Add src/pages/Login.js: form with email/password/rememberMe
- Add src/pages/QuestionList.js: paginated list with search, category
  filter, sort selector, FloatButton CTA
- Add src/pages/QuestionDetail.js: question view with answers, likes,
  dislikes, favorites, collapsible comments, inline edit/delete
- Add src/pages/AskQuestion.js: form with tags-mode category select
- Add src/pages/Profile.js: 5-tab personal center (questions, answers,
  favorites, appeals with submit form, notifications with mark-read)
- Add src/pages/admin/AdminConsole.js: 6-tab admin console (stats cards,
  question/answer/user/appeal management with reason modals, operation logs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Node.js + Express + MongoDB backend for anonymous Q&A platform

- JWT auth with single-session enforcement via activeToken
- Models: User, Question, Answer, Comment, OperationLog, Appeal, Notification
- Auth middleware: authenticate + optionalAuth
- Role-based access: requireRole, requireOwnerOrAdmin
- Controllers: auth, questions, answers, users, admin
- Rate limiting: global 200/15min, auth 20/15min
- Soft-delete for answers/comments, like/dislike toggle
- Admin ops: archive, ban, pin, category change — all logged + notified

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Node.js + Express + MongoDB backend for anonymous Q&A platform

- 7 Mongoose models: User, Question, Answer, Comment, OperationLog, Appeal, Notification
- JWT auth with single-session enforcement (activeToken) and optionalAuth middleware
- Role-based access control (user / moderator / admin)
- Full CRUD for questions and answers with soft-delete support
- Like/dislike toggle, favorites, comments, nested notifications
- Admin moderation: archive, pin, ban/unban users, change category
- Operation audit log and pending appeal resolution
- Global (200 req/15 min) and auth-specific (20 req/15 min) rate limiting via express-rate-limit
- Two-router export pattern (questionAnswersRouter + answerRouter) for nested routes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add filing/capital-markets caches and summaries

Add DB migrations, models, services and API support for filing and capital-markets event caching and summaries.

- Alembic migrations: add beneficial_ownership_party detail fields, filing_events and capital_markets_events caches, and 13f manager/universe and amendment flags.
- Models: export new FilingEvent and CapitalMarketsEvent; update BeneficialOwnershipParty with new indexed fields.
- Services: add capital_markets, filing events, proxy parsing, and beneficial ownership collectors; update sec_edgar integration and cache query usage.
- API: new summary endpoints and payloads for institutional holdings, beneficial ownership, capital markets, filing events and governance; reuse cached rows when available and fall back to live SEC parsing.
- Serialization/helpers: added normalizers and summary builders for filings, capital markets, governance, beneficial ownership and institutional holdings; proxy parsing integration for governance signals.
- Tests: add unit tests for beneficial ownership, capital markets, eight-k events, proxy parser and related coverage updates.

These changes introduce caching and summary layers to reduce live SEC fetches and provide compact summary responses for front-end pages.

Co-Authored-By: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize string handling - interning, fast comparison, concat

- Pre-seed intern pool with ~30 well-known JS property names (length,
  prototype, constructor, etc.) to avoid first-use allocation misses
- Add str_eq_fast() for O(1) pointer-based string equality
- Optimize JsValue PartialEq and is_strictly_equal with Rc::ptr_eq
  fast path before falling back to O(n) content comparison
- Convert 8 dispatch handlers from direct constant pool String::clone
  to get_string_constant() which interns + caches Rc<str> per frame:
  StaGlobal, DeleteLookupSlot, StaLookupSlot, LdaLookupSlot,
  LdaLookupSlotInsideTypeof, LdaLookupContextSlot(InsideTypeof),
  LdaLookupGlobalSlot(InsideTypeof), LdaNamedPropertyFromSuper
- Re-seed well-known names on clear_intern_pool() for test isolation

fix: sync `class_names` from dataset after training so `predict()` returns correct labels (#816)

* sync class_names from dataset after training so predict() returns correct labels (#509)

After trainer.fit(), RFDETR.train() now syncs RFDETRDataModule.class_names
back to self.model.class_names so that predict() returns dataset labels
instead of falling through to COCO defaults.

BestModelCallback._save_checkpoint() now enriches TrainConfig with dataset
class names (regular and EMA paths) before saving, so reloaded checkpoints
also carry the correct label mapping.

Five regression tests added covering both the in-memory sync and the
checkpoint serialisation paths.

- Updated `class_names` checks to use `is None` instead of truthiness in multiple paths (e.g., training sync, checkpoint saving) to avoid overwriting empty lists.
- Added regression tests validating that explicit `class_names=[]` are not replaced by dataset defaults (regular and EMA checkpoint paths).
- Use `getattr` for `config` and `datamodule` to avoid potential attribute errors during `class_names` sync.

perf: re-enable global cache with generation counter

The per-frame global variable cache was disabled because nested function
calls could mutate global_env without invalidating the caller's cache.

Fix: wrap global_env HashMap in GlobalEnv struct with a generation counter
that increments on every mutation. Cache lookups compare their snapshot
generation against the current one and fall back to HashMap on mismatch.

Restores cache hit path for hot global reads while maintaining correctness
across nested calls, setters, and callbacks.
